title: ML/LLM Interview Companion
subtitle: Prep for Top-Tier ML & LLM Engineering Interviews
tagline: A focused companion to The LLM Stack: frameworks, model answers, worked system-design cases, and a study plan for ML/LLM interview loops at big-tech and frontier labs.
author: Companion to The LLM Stack

chapters.title: The ML Engineer Interview: Formats & What's Tested
chapters.scope: What top-tier ML/LLM interview loops actually test across companies (ML breadth + depth, ML system design, coding, project deep-dive), the common rubrics, how to drive the conversation, signals interviewers look for, time management, common failure modes. Use Google's ML domain round and the FAANG/frontier-lab loops as concrete examples. Strategic overview.

chapters.title: ML Breadth: Rapid-Fire Concepts & Model Answers
chapters.scope: A curated bank of breadth Q&A: bias-variance, regularization, overfitting, optimization, batchnorm/dropout, transformers, attention, embeddings, evaluation metrics, class imbalance, gradient issues — each with a crisp 'model answer'. Dense and high-yield.

chapters.title: ML System Design: A Framework
chapters.scope: A repeatable framework: clarify -> requirements/SLOs -> data -> features -> model -> training -> eval -> serving -> monitoring -> iteration. The structure to impose on any ML-design prompt, with checklists and tradeoff talking points.

chapters.title: ML System Design: Worked Cases
chapters.scope: Fully worked designs: (1) a recommendation/ranking system, (2) search/semantic retrieval, (3) an LLM serving platform, (4) a RAG assistant, (5) a content-moderation system, (6) a fine-tuning/RLHF pipeline. Each end-to-end using the framework. The centerpiece prep chapter.

chapters.title: Coding for ML Interviews
chapters.scope: Likely coding: implement attention/softmax/k-means/logistic-regression/backprop from scratch, plus DS&A staples (arrays, hashing, two-pointers, DP, graphs) framed for ML candidates; numpy fluency. Runnable solutions. Practice-oriented.

chapters.title: LLM-Specific Deep-Dive Questions
chapters.scope: The questions a strong LLM candidate must nail: explain attention/RoPE/KV-cache/flash-attention, why GQA, DPO vs PPO, GRPO, quantization, scaling laws, MoE, speculative decoding, RLHF, hallucination causes — with model answers cross-linked to chapters.

chapters.title: Behavioral, Leadership & Project Deep-Dive
chapters.scope: STAR method, the project deep-dive (your RL/LLM work), leadership signals top labs and big-tech look for (ownership, collaboration, impact), handling 'tell me about a hard bug', impact framing, questions to ask. Rounding out the loop.

chapters.title: The 10-Day Study Plan & Final Checklist
chapters.scope: A concrete day-by-day plan (mapping to this book's chapters) for the 10 days before an ML/LLM interview, a spaced-repetition flashcard set of must-knows, a final-48-hours checklist, and a 'reset your nerves' page. Actionable and motivating.
